title: Warm Aerated Indian Potato Sandwich
description: A cozy Indian-inspired sandwich made from roasted potato buns, aerated yogurt filling, and aromatic spices, perfect for a low-calorie breakfast.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Peel and slice potatoes into 1/4-inch thick rounds, then pat dry.
Place potato slices on the prepared sheet and brush lightly with olive oil; bake for 20 minutes until golden and tender.
While potatoes roast, whisk yogurt, ginger, cilantro, lemon juice, cumin, garam masala, and baking powder together in a bowl.
Remove roasted potato slices, let cool slightly, then mash each slice with a fork until a smooth paste forms.
Fold the yogurt mixture into the mashed potato paste, seasoning with salt and pepper to taste, and stir until well combined.
Scoop the mixture onto a clean surface, then roll into small discs about 3 inches in diameter using a lightly floured surface.
Place the discs onto the parchment sheet, then bake at 200°C for an additional 12-15 minutes until firm and lightly browned.
Slice the baked potato buns in half, fill with a thin spread of the aerated yogurt mixture, and serve warm.
